chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. Nos.D-6449/2020 and 1900/2019 _______________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judges _______________________________________________________________ 08/04/2021: Malik Altaf Hussain, Advocate for the petitioner in C.P. No.D-6449 of 2020. Mr. Ali Asadullah Bullo, Advocate for the petitioners in C.P. No.D-1900 of 2019. Mr. Muhammad Nishat Warsi, Deputy Attorney General for Pakistan for the respondents. ------------------------------- These petitions have been filed on the ground that the petitioners have not been given their pensionary and other benefits. Notices thereafter were issued to the respondents and in response thereto commence have been filed. It was admitted by the respondents that certain portions of amount have been received on account of capitalized value of pension by the petitioners and with regard to the other benefits including the pension payable by the Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan (DRAP) a board would be constituted which would decide the fate of the pensions and other benefits payable to the petitioners in accordance with law. We have heard both the learned counsel at considerable length and with their consent dispose of the instant petitions in the following terms:- 1) That the Respondent No.2 would release the admitted amount of pensionary benefits, if any, of the petitioner(s), which was received by them on account of Capitalized Value of Pension within fifteen days from the date of receipt of this order. 2 2) That a Board would be constituted, if not constituted previously, which would convene its meeting within one month’s time from the date of receipt of this order and would decide the matter pertaining to pensionary and other benefits accruing to the employees/ petitioners strictly in accordance with law.

It was admitted by the respondents that certain portions of amount have been received on account of capitalized value of pension by the petitioners and with regard to the other benefits including the pension payable by the Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan (DRAP) a board would be constituted which would decide the fate of the pensions and other benefits payable to the petitioners in accordance with law. We have heard both the learned counsel at considerable length and with their consent dispose of the instant petitions in the following terms:- 1) That the Respondent No.2 would release the admitted amount of pensionary benefits, if any, of the petitioner(s), which was received by them on account of Capitalized Value of Pension within fifteen days from the date of receipt of this order. 2 2) That a Board would be constituted, if not constituted previously, which would convene its meeting within one month’s time from the date of receipt of this order and would decide the matter pertaining to pensionary and other benefits accruing to the employees/ petitioners strictly in accordance with law. In terms of the above directions, both the instant petitions alongwith the listed application(s) stand disposed of. Compliance report be submitted to the MIT-II of this Court. JUDGE JUDGE S.Akhtar
